name: NeuroLink v2
tagline: The AI-native learning platform for India's CS and AI universities.
description: Turns any lecture into an interactive, personalised experience. See every student's knowledge graph in real time. Zero GPU infrastructure required.
longDescription: India admits 3.9 lakh computer-science engineering students every year; most graduate without building a real model. NeuroLink v2 gives each of them a live Concept DNA knowledge graph, a personalised lesson from every PDF their professor uploads, and a production-ready sandbox — with no GPU management.
forWhom: For university deans, CS and AI programme heads, and research centres.

keyCapabilities.title: Concept DNA
keyCapabilities.description: A live, per-student knowledge graph powered by Neo4j — visible to students, professors, and institutional leadership.

keyCapabilities.title: Ghost Mode reasoning
keyCapabilities.description: Students watch an AI think through a problem step-by-step, exposing the scaffolding behind expert reasoning.

keyCapabilities.title: Paper-to-Playground
keyCapabilities.description: Turns research papers into interactive sandboxes — read a paper, then run its experiments on the same page.

keyCapabilities.title: Zero GPU infrastructure
keyCapabilities.description: Runs entirely on cloud APIs. No CUDA drivers, no GPU clusters, no ops team required.

outcomes: Students graduate with a portfolio of real experiments, not just grades
Professors teach with live visibility into every learner's understanding
Universities attract top students with infrastructure that rivals the best globally
Research becomes reproducible the first time it is taught
